_comment: Copy to relay-e.config.json and customize. Secrets stay in .env via ${ENV_VAR} substitution. Restart the API to pick up changes (skill-as-config from the DB lands later — see roadmap).

connectors._comment: POSTGRES — connect to the customer's Postgres. Schema is introspected on boot and injected into the LLM prompt so the model knows what's queryable. Use a READ-ONLY DB role in production; the engine also enforces SELECT-only at the SQL layer as defense in depth.
connectors.name: Shop DB

connectors._comment: MYSQL — same pattern, different driver. Run `npm install mysql2 -w @relay-e/engine` first.
connectors.name: Analytics MySQL

config.description: Read replica of the analytics warehouse.

connectors._comment: MONGODB — schema inferred by sampling N docs per collection. Run `npm install mongodb -w @relay-e/engine` first.
connectors.name: Events Mongo

config.description: User event stream (clicks, page views, conversions).

connectors._comment: HTTP — wrap any REST API behind one base URL. If you supply openApiUrl, the engine pulls the spec on boot and surfaces every endpoint to the LLM in the prompt — model writes its own calls.
connectors.name: Stripe

config.description: Stripe API for billing and customer lookups.

connectors._comment: WEB SEARCH — Tavily / Brave / Serper. Gives the LLM the ability to look up current information.
connectors.name: Web Search

skills.name: shop-assistant
skills.description: Customer support for the e-commerce shop. Looks up orders in the DB, calls Stripe for billing, falls back to web search for general questions.
skills.systemPrompt: You are the shop's support assistant. Answer questions about orders, customers, and billing using the connectors below. For order/customer questions, query the shop_db. For billing/refunds, use Stripe. For anything outside our system (return policies of competitors, current shipping carrier news), use web_search and cite the URL. Never invent data — if a tool returns nothing, say so.

skills.name: analyst
skills.description: Data analyst that answers ad-hoc questions over the analytics warehouse.
skills.systemPrompt: You are a senior data analyst. The user will ask questions about the business. Write SQL against the analytics_db connector to answer them — start by exploring the schema if you're unsure. Always show your reasoning briefly, then the answer with key numbers.

skills.name: general-assistant
skills.description: Default helpful assistant with web search.
skills.systemPrompt: You are a helpful general-purpose assistant. Use web_search for anything that requires current information.
